root_text.iti22:2.2: Sukhassetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhivacanaṁ iṭṭhassa kantassa piyassa manāpassa yadidaṁ puññāni.
root_text.iti22:2.3: Abhijānāmi kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, dīgharattaṁ katānaṁ puññānaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ piyaṁ manāpaṁ vipākaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ.
root_text.iti22:2.4: Satta vassāni mettacittaṁ bhāvetvā satta saṁvaṭṭavivaṭṭakappe nayimaṁ lokaṁ punarāgamāsiṁ.
root_text.iti22:2.5: Saṁvaṭṭamāne sudaṁ, bhikkhave, kappe ābhassarūpago homi;
root_text.iti22:3.1: Tatra sudaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmā homi mahābrahmā abhibhū anabhibhūto aññadatthudaso vasavattī.
root_text.iti22:3.2: Chattiṁsakkhattuṁ kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sakko ahosiṁ devānamindo;
root_text.iti22:3.3: anekasatakkhattuṁ rājā ahosiṁ cakkavattī dhammiko dhammarājā cāturanto vijitāvī janapadatthāvariyappatto sattaratanasamannāgato.
root_text.iti22:4.2: ‘kissa nu kho me idaṁ kammassa phalaṁ, kissa kammassa vipāko, yenāhaṁ etarahi evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo’ti?
root_text.iti22:4.4: ‘tiṇṇaṁ kho me idaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ, tiṇṇaṁ kammānaṁ vipāko, yenāhaṁ etarahi evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvoti, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.iti22:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti22:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti22:2.2: For ‘good deeds’ is a term for happiness, for what is likable, desirable, and agreeable.
translation_text.iti22:2.3: I recall undergoing for a long time the likable, desirable, and agreeable results of good deeds performed over a long time.
translation_text.iti22:2.4: Having developed a mind of love for seven years, for seven eons of the cosmos contracting and expanding I didn’t return to this world again.
translation_text.iti22:2.5: As the eon contracted I went to the realm of streaming radiance.
translation_text.iti22:2.6: As it expanded I was reborn in an empty mansion of divinity.
translation_text.iti22:3.1: There I was the Divinity, the Great Divinity, the vanquisher, the unvanquished, the universal seer, the wielder of power.
translation_text.iti22:3.2: I was Sakka, lord of gods, thirty-six times.
translation_text.iti22:3.3: Many hundreds of times I was a king, a wheel-turning monarch, a just and principled king. My dominion extended to all four sides, I achieved stability in the country, and I possessed the seven treasures.
translation_text.iti22:4.2: ‘Of what deed of mine is this the fruit and result, that I am now so mighty and powerful?’
translation_text.iti22:4.4: ‘It is the fruit and result of three kinds of deeds:
translation_text.iti22:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti22:6.3: Abyāpajjaṁ → abyāpajjhaṁ (bj, pts-vp-pli1, csp1ed); abyābajjhaṁ (an4.40:1 [40. Udāyīsutta]; iti60:1 [Puññakiriyavatthusutta])
